name: InfluxDB 3 MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official InfluxDB 3 MCP server enabling AI agents to query and write time-series data — running Flux and SQL queries against InfluxDB, writing metrics/events, managing buckets, and integrating with IoT and observability pipelines.
use_cases: Querying time-series metrics for infrastructure monitoring agents
Writing sensor data and IoT telemetry from automation agents
Analyzing trends and anomalies in time-series data from analytics agents
Querying Telegraf-collected metrics for DevOps agents
Building dashboards by querying InfluxDB metrics from Grafana-adjacent agents
Managing retention policies and continuous queries from data lifecycle agents
not_for: Teams using Prometheus/VictoriaMetrics for metrics storage
Document or key-value storage use cases
best_when: An agent needs to query or write time-series data — especially for IoT, infrastructure metrics, financial data, or observability pipelines.
avoid_when: You're using Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics, or another time-series database.

auth.notes: InfluxDB API tokens with bucket-level read/write and org-level permissions. All-access tokens and custom-scoped tokens available.

pricing.notes: InfluxDB OSS is free open source. InfluxDB Cloud is usage-based. InfluxDB 3 (IOx) is the newest version with SQL support. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits vary by tier; Cloud has write rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: InfluxDB 1.x (InfluxQL), 2.x (Flux), and 3.x (SQL) have different query languages
Organization and bucket hierarchy must be known before querying
Timestamps are nanosecond precision by default — agents must handle carefully
Line protocol format required for writes — different from SQL INSERT
Tag vs field distinction is critical — tags are indexed, fields are not
Data retention policies delete old data automatically — agents must account for this
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Cloud). Token-based auth with bucket-level scoping. SOC 2, GDPR. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Cloud). Bucket-level token scoping. SOC 2, GDPR. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.
